access: Shared privately with family and friends.

items.name: MateFC
items.description: A bilingual, phone-framed mock of a soccer-school parent and coach portal, covering membership tiers, enrolment, attendance, and billing.
items.benefit: Lets the school preview a complete parent-portal experience before committing to a paid platform.

detail.tagline: A bilingual mock of a soccer-school parent & coach portal.
overview: MateFC is a phone-framed web app that mocks the full experience of a soccer school's membership platform, in both English and Japanese.

It models two role flows — parents/students and coach/admin — across enrolment, attendance, billing, and academy/trial membership tiers, all driven by realistic static data so the whole journey can be demoed without a backend.
highlights: Bilingual UI (English / 日本語) with one-tap language switching
Separate Parent/Student portal and Coach/Admin dashboard
Membership tiers — Regular, Academy and Trial — each styled distinctly
Trial-to-membership conversion, attendance, news and billing flows
Built with React, TypeScript and Vite

items.name: English Vocabulary
items.description: An offline-capable vocabulary PWA with flashcards, quizzes, and a themed wiki of 412 words collected since 2020.
items.benefit: Turns years of scattered vocabulary notes into a structured study tool that works on any device.

detail.tagline: A vocabulary PWA built from years of personal notes.
overview: An offline-capable progressive web app that turns 412 words collected since 2020 into a structured study tool.
It pairs a flashcard and quiz trainer with a themed wiki — business, idioms, Australian slang, medical, legal and social English — and signs in with Google so progress follows you across devices.
highlights: 412 words spanning 2020–2026
Flashcards, quizzes and progress statistics
Themed wiki: business, idioms, Aussie slang, medical, legal, social
Installable PWA — works offline on phone or desktop
Google sign-in

items.name: Block Survival
items.description: A Minecraft-flavoured, third-person wave-survival shooter built with Three.js in a single zero-build HTML file.
items.benefit: Gives the family an install-free game that runs in any browser, on desktop or iPad.

detail.tagline: A Minecraft-flavoured wave-survival shooter in the browser.
overview: A third-person, Minecraft-styled survival shooter built with Three.js in a single zero-build HTML file.
Players fight endless waves of mobs, earn tokens from kills, and upgrade from a pistol all the way to a diamond cannon — playable with keyboard and mouse or with full on-screen iPad controls.
highlights: Endless waves: zombies, skeletons, creepers, spiders, endermen and slimes
Weapon progression: Pistol → SMG → Rifle → Shotgun → Diamond Cannon
Stat upgrades for HP, damage, reload and move speed
Keyboard/mouse and full iPad touch controls
Single-file Three.js, no build step

items.name: Anzac of Zombies
items.description: A two-player co-op browser game where kids climb a school tower kicking soccer balls at a cartoon zombie horde.
items.benefit: Gives two kids a shared, firearm-free co-op game across iPads on home Wi-Fi, with no app-store install.

detail.tagline: A two-player co-op game made for the kids.
overview: A kid-friendly, two-player co-op browser game where players climb the floors of their school, kicking soccer balls at a goofy zombie horde, collecting coins, upgrading gear and beating rooftop bosses.
It runs on two iPads over the home Wi-Fi via a small Node WebSocket server — no firearms, just soccer balls.
highlights: Local two-player co-op over home Wi-Fi
Climb floors, beat bosses, upgrade soccer gear
Kid-friendly — soccer balls instead of weapons
Runs on iPads via Add to Home Screen
Node.js WebSocket server with a canvas client

items.name: Aussie Ramenman
items.description: A daily Python script that gathers tagged ramen posts from Sydney and Melbourne Instagram accounts into a Notion digest for manual review.
items.benefit: Surfaces fresh, relevant posts to engage with each morning, with no against-ToS automation on the account.

detail.tagline: A daily ramen-post digest delivered into Notion.
overview: A Python automation that, once a day, scans the tagged feeds of around 21 Sydney and Melbourne ramen accounts and writes the fresh posts into a Notion database for manual review each morning.
It deliberately performs no likes, comments or follows — it only gathers and reports, keeping the account safe from automation bans.
highlights: Scans ~21 Sydney/Melbourne ramen accounts daily
Writes fresh posts to a Notion database
Dedupes via local SQLite and auto-archives old entries
No likes, comments or follows — ToS-safe by design
Scheduled with launchd; Python + Notion API

items.name: Minecraft Kanji Quest
items.description: A daily kanji practice quiz styled after Minecraft.
items.benefit: Makes routine kanji drilling more engaging for kids through a familiar game aesthetic.

detail.tagline: A daily kanji quiz with a Minecraft look.
overview: A browser-based daily kanji practice quiz styled after Minecraft, designed to make routine character drilling feel like a game for kids.
highlights: Daily kanji practice quiz
Minecraft-inspired visual style
Quick, repeatable drills for kids
